schema.description: A server for accessing fruit information and nutritional data from the Fruityvice API, providing details about various fruits and their nutritional content.

tools.name: list_fruits
tools.description: Retrieve a list of all available fruits from the Fruityvice database.

limit.description: Maximum number of fruit results to return. If not specified, all available fruits are returned.

fruits.description: List of available fruits from the Fruityvice database.

name.description: The common name of the fruit (e.g., 'apple', 'banana', 'orange').

tools.name: get_fruit_nutrition
tools.description: Retrieve detailed nutritional information for a specific fruit by name.

fruit_name.description: The common name of the fruit to look up (e.g., 'apple', 'banana', 'orange').

name.description: The common name of the fruit (e.g., 'apple', 'banana', 'orange').

nutritions.description: Nutritional breakdown of the fruit per 100 grams of edible portion.

calories.description: Energy content in kilocalories (kcal) per 100g of the fruit.

fat.description: Total fat content in grams per 100g of the fruit.

sugar.description: Total sugar content in grams per 100g of the fruit.

carbohydrates.description: Total carbohydrate content in grams per 100g of the fruit.

protein.description: Total protein content in grams per 100g of the fruit.

scenarios.description: Basic scenario with 2 fruits and default nutrition data for common fruits
Medium complexity with 5 diverse fruits including tropical and citrus varieties
Complex scenario with 8 fruits covering wide nutritional range from low-calorie citrus to high-fat avocado and coconut
Boundary case with empty fruits list and minimal nutrition map to test edge case handling

test_cases.description: List all fruits without limit
List fruits with limit=1
List fruits with limit=10 (boundary case, exceeds available)
Get nutrition for apple
Get nutrition for banana
Get nutrition for non-existent fruit (error case)
test_cases.note: Expected error - tool correctly rejects fruit not in scenario

save_scenario.details: Saved scenario matches original scenario exactly. All state preserved correctly.

test_cases.description: Boundary case: limit=0 (should return empty list)
Valid case: no limit (should return all fruits)
Valid case: limit=3 (should return first 3 fruits)
Valid case: get nutrition for strawberry
Valid case: get nutrition for mango
Valid case: get nutrition for kiwi
Error case: fruit not found in scenario
test_cases.note: Expected error - correctly rejected invalid fruit name

save_scenario.details: Saved scenario matches original scenario exactly. All fruits and nutrition data preserved correctly.

test_cases.description: List all fruits without limit
List fruits with limit=3
List fruits with limit=0 (boundary case)
Get nutrition for pomegranate (case-insensitive test)
test_cases.output: Successfully returned nutrition data with calories=83.0, fat=1.2, sugar=14.0, carbohydrates=19.0, protein=1.7

test_cases.description: Get nutrition for avocado (valid case)
test_cases.output: Successfully returned nutrition data with calories=160.0, fat=15.0, sugar=0.7, carbohydrates=9.0, protein=2.0

test_cases.description: Get nutrition for cherry (valid case)
test_cases.output: Successfully returned nutrition data with calories=63.0, fat=0.2, sugar=13.0, carbohydrates=16.0, protein=1.1

test_cases.description: Get nutrition for non-existent fruit (error handling test)
test_cases.output: Correctly rejected with error: Fruit 'mango' not found

save_scenario.details: Saved scenario matches original scenario exactly. All 8 fruits and their complete nutritional data preserved correctly.

test_cases.description: List fruits with limit=0 (boundary case)
test_cases.expected: Empty list since fruits array is empty

test_cases.description: List all fruits without limit (valid case)
test_cases.expected: Empty list since fruits array is empty

test_cases.description: List fruits with limit=1 (valid case)
test_cases.expected: Empty list since fruits array is empty

test_cases.description: Get nutrition for existing fruit (valid case)
Get nutrition for non-existent fruit (error case)
Get nutrition with different case (boundary case)

save_scenario.details: Saved scenario matches original scenario exactly. State remained consistent throughout all operations.
